methods.description: Recording environmental parameters (precipitation, temperature, relative humidity) and collecting rainwater samples.

steps.description: The amount of precipitation and air temperature outside of the cave were recorded by a Hobo Pendant Event Data Logger (model UA-003-64)
steps.searchable_summary: record precipitation and outside air temperature using data logger

steps.description: temperature and relative humidity inside the cave were recorded by a Hobo Temperature/Relative Humidity Data Logger (model U23-001)
steps.searchable_summary: record inside cave temperature and relative humidity using data logger

steps.description: Rainwater for isotopic analysis was sampled every 15 days in a water collector installed together with the rain gauge.

methods.description: Analysis of stable isotopes (δ¹⁸O and δD) in water samples.

steps.description: The analyses for δ 18 O and δ D of water were performed using a Picarro L2120-i Analyzer at the Stable Isotope Laboratory of the University of Brasilia.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze water samples for δ¹⁸O and δD using isotope analyzer

steps.description: The data are reported as Vienna Standard Mean Ocean Water (VSMOW) standard with precision of ± 0.1‰ (δ 18 O) and ± 0.5 (δ D).
steps.searchable_summary: report water isotope data relative to VSMOW standard

methods.description: Analysis of stable oxygen isotopes (δ¹⁸O) in carbonate speleothem samples.

steps.description: For each measurement, approximately 100 μ g of powder was drilled from the sample and analyzed with an on-line, automated, carbonate preparation system linked to a Thermo-Finnigan Delta Plus Advantage at the Centro de Pesquisas Geocronológicas of the Geosciences Institute of Universidade de São Paulo (CPGeo-IGc-USP).
steps.searchable_summary: drill powder from speleothem sample and analyze using automated preparation system and mass spectrometer
inputs: Thermo-Finnigan Delta Plus Advantage mass spectrometer

steps.description: The oxygen isotope ratios are expressed in δ notation, the per mil deviation from the Vienna Peedee Belemite (VPDB) standard.
steps.searchable_summary: express carbonate oxygen isotope ratios in δ notation relative to VPDB standard

methods.description: Dating stalagmites using the Uranium-Thorium method.

steps.description: The powder carbonate samples (~100 mg) on stalagmites were drilled using a carbide dental drill following stratigraphic horizons.
steps.searchable_summary: drill powder samples from stalagmites following stratigraphic horizons

steps.description: To separate uranium and thorium the chemical procedure described in Edwards et al. 29 was applied.
steps.searchable_summary: apply chemical procedure to separate uranium and thorium
inputs: chemical reagents (as per Edwards et al. 29)

steps.description: After the separation of uranium and thorium each sample was dried and diluted for injection into the spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: dry and dilute separated U and Th samples
outputs: dried and diluted U and Th samples

steps.description: The analysis was performed using a multi-collector inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry technique in a MC-ICP-MS, Thermo-Finnigan NEPTUNE, according to the techniques described in Cheng et al. 30 .
steps.searchable_summary: analyze U and Th samples using MC-ICP-MS
inputs: dried and diluted U and Th samples
